# Research Results

## 2026-03-28

### Databricks
- **[Databricks Launches Lakewatch SIEM, Acquires Antimatter and SiftD.ai]** — Databricks entered the security market with Lakewatch, a new open, agentic SIEM product powered by Anthropic's Claude for AI-driven threat detection and investigation. The launch was underpinned by the acquisitions of two startups: Antimatter (data control plane for secure agent deployment) and SiftD.ai (agentic automation for security engineering, founded by ex-Splunk engineers). (Source: [TechCrunch](https://techcrunch.com/2026/03/24/databricks-buys-two-startups-lakewatch-antimatter-siftd-ai-security/), 2026-03-24)
- **[Databricks Acquires Quotient AI for Agent Evaluations]** — Databricks acquired Quotient AI, a Boston-based startup specializing in evaluation and reinforcement learning for AI agents. Quotient's platform helps enterprises monitor agent behavior in production, detect critical issues, and continuously improve agent performance. (Source: [Databricks Blog](https://www.databricks.com/blog/databricks-acquires-quotient-ai-power-ai-agent-evaluations), 2026-03-24)
- **[Lakeflow Connect Free Tier Announced at FabCon 2026]** — Databricks introduced a permanent free tier for Lakeflow Connect, providing 100 DBUs per day per workspace at no cost — enough to ingest approximately 100 million records daily. The free tier covers nine major database connectors and popular SaaS applications, representing ~$12,775/year in savings per workspace. (Source: [SunnyData](https://www.sunnydata.ai/blog/lakeflow-connect-free-tier-etl-cost-savings), 2026-03-18)
- **[Genie Code Launches Agentic Engineering for Data Work]** — Databricks launched Genie Code on March 11, bringing agentic engineering capabilities to data work. The Sample Data Explorer with Genie Code is now GA, letting users ask natural language questions about tables in Unity Catalog. (Source: [Databricks Release Notes](https://docs.databricks.com/aws/en/release-notes/product/2026/march), 2026-03-11)
- **[Unity Catalog: Governed Tags GA, New Discover Experience Beta]** — Governed Tags became generally available, allowing administrators to define controlled tag sets for Unity Catalog objects. A new Discover experience entered Beta, unifying data discovery with embedded business context, trust signals, and governed access directly within Unity Catalog. (Source: [Databricks Release Notes](https://docs.databricks.com/aws/en/release-notes/product/2026/march), 2026-03-28)

### AI Innovation
- **[OpenAI Releases GPT-5.4 in Three Variants]** — OpenAI launched GPT-5.4 on March 5 in Standard, Thinking (reasoning-first), and Pro (maximum capability) variants. The model supports context windows up to 1.05 million tokens and reduces factual claim errors by 33% compared to GPT-5.2. (Source: [LLM Stats](https://llm-stats.com/llm-updates), 2026-03-05)
- **[DeepSeek V4 Launches with 1 Trillion Parameters]** — DeepSeek released V4 around March 3, hitting 1 trillion parameters while using only 32 billion active parameters per token via mixture-of-experts architecture — fewer active parameters than V3 despite being vastly larger. (Source: [BuildFastWithAI](https://www.buildfastwithai.com/blogs/ai-models-march-2026-releases), 2026-03-03)
- **[NVIDIA Announces Agent Toolkit and OpenShell at GTC 2026]** — At GTC 2026 (March 10–14), NVIDIA announced its Agent Toolkit including the open-source OpenShell runtime for building self-evolving agents. Leading platforms including Adobe, Atlassian, Salesforce, and ServiceNow are adopting the toolkit for enterprise agent deployments. (Source: [NVIDIA Newsroom](https://nvidianews.nvidia.com/news/ai-agents), 2026-03-16)
- **[Xiaomi's MiMo-V2-Pro Revealed as Mystery 1T Parameter Model]** — A mystery model called Hunter Alpha appeared on OpenRouter on March 11 with jaw-dropping specs — 1 trillion parameters, 1M token context, agent-focused, and free. On March 18, it was revealed to be MiMo-V2-Pro, built by Xiaomi's AI division. (Source: [DevFlokers](https://www.devflokers.com/blog/new-ai-model-releases-open-source-projects-march-18-19-2026), 2026-03-18)
- **[OpenAI Discontinues Sora Public API]** — OpenAI quietly announced the discontinuation of the Sora public API on March 24, citing unsustainable economics of video generation at scale. This marks a notable strategic retreat from the video generation market. (Source: [Digital Applied](https://www.digitalapplied.com/blog/march-2026-ai-roundup-month-that-changed-everything), 2026-03-24)
- **[Model Context Protocol Crosses 97 Million Installs]** — MCP crossed 97 million installs in March 2026, signaling its transition from experimental standard to foundational agentic infrastructure. Every major AI provider now ships MCP-compatible tooling. (Source: [Digital Applied](https://www.digitalapplied.com/blog/march-2026-ai-roundup-month-that-changed-everything), 2026-03-28)

### AI Governance
- **[White House Releases National Policy Framework for AI]** — On March 20, the White House released a comprehensive AI policy framework with seven pillars including child safety, IP protection, free speech, innovation, and notably a push for federal preemption of state AI laws. The framework recommends relying on existing regulators rather than creating a new federal AI authority. (Source: [WilmerHale](https://www.wilmerhale.com/en/insights/blogs/wilmerhale-privacy-and-cybersecurity-law/20260323-white-house-releases-national-policy-framework-for-artificial-intelligence), 2026-03-20)
- **[EU Council Agrees Position on AI Act Simplification]** — The EU Council agreed its position on proposals to streamline AI rules as part of the Omnibus VII package, including potentially delaying high-risk AI system obligations by up to 16 months and extending SME exemptions to small mid-caps. A new provision prohibiting AI-generated non-consensual sexual content was also added. (Source: [EU Council](https://www.consilium.europa.eu/en/press/press-releases/2026/03/13/council-agrees-position-to-streamline-rules-on-artificial-intelligence/), 2026-03-13)
- **[NIST Launches AI Agent Standards Initiative]** — NIST launched an initiative focused on interoperability, identity management, and security controls for AI agent systems, with public comments on the RFI due March 9 and listening sessions through April 2026. This signals increased federal focus on governing autonomous AI agents. (Source: [Pillsbury Law](https://www.pillsburylaw.com/en/news-and-insights/nist-ai-agent-standards.html), 2026-03-09)
- **[GSA and NIST Partner on AI Security Evaluation]** — GSA and NIST formed a new partnership to improve government AI management, creating evaluation guidelines and checklists for agencies to test AI systems before real-world deployment. The Center for AI Standards will provide tooling to evaluate advanced AI models. (Source: [Federal News Network](https://federalnewsnetwork.com/federal-newscast/2026/03/dod-attempts-to-make-its-case-for-anthropics-label-as-national-security-risk/), 2026-03-28)
- **[Global Audit Study: 66% Want Harmonized AI Framework]** — A Caseware/IDC global study of 1,000+ audit professionals found 66% believe there is an urgent need for a globally harmonized AI framework for audit and assurance, 55% would trade AI performance for stronger safety measures, and 79% rate algorithmic bias risk as moderately to extremely significant. (Source: [GlobeNewsWire](https://www.globenewswire.com/news-release/2026/03/10/3252844/0/en/AI-Is-Reshaping-Audit-and-Accounting-On-a-Foundation-of-Trust-and-Responsibility.html), 2026-03-10)

### Data Governance
- **[Trump Administration Issues New Cyber Strategy and Executive Order]** — On March 6, the White House released the Cyber Strategy for America alongside an Executive Order targeting cyber-enabled crime, articulating a new doctrine centered on imposing costs on adversaries and mobilizing government and private-sector capabilities at scale. (Source: [Sidley Austin](https://datamatters.sidley.com/2026/03/10/the-new-cyber-doctrine-of-the-united-states-the-trump-administration-issues-cyber-strategy-and-executive-order-targeting-cybercrime/), 2026-03-06)
- **[TELUS Digital Confirms 1 Petabyte Breach by ShinyHunterz]** — TELUS Digital confirmed on March 11 it was attacked by the ShinyHunterz ransomware group, compromising 1 petabyte of data including BPO customer data, source codes, FBI background checks, financial records, and voice recordings. (Source: [Privacy Guides](https://www.privacyguides.org/news/2026/03/27/data-breach-roundup-mar-20-26-2026/), 2026-03-11)
- **[Navia Benefit Solutions Breach Affects 2.7 Million Individuals]** — Hackers accessed Navia Benefit Solutions' network over three weeks between December 2025 and January 2026, affecting nearly 2.7 million individuals. Notification letters began mailing on March 18, 2026. (Source: [HIPAA Journal](https://www.hipaajournal.com/navia-benefit-solutions-data-breach/), 2026-03-18)
- **[State AGs Accelerate Data Privacy Enforcement on National Security Grounds]** — State attorneys general in Florida, Texas, and Arizona are increasingly asserting authority over sensitive personal data access, with national security concerns becoming a new basis for enforcement activity at the state level. (Source: [Davis Wright Tremaine](https://www.dwt.com/blogs/privacy--security-law-blog/2026/03/trust-issues-march-2026), 2026-03-28)
- **[Heritage Bank Reports Data Breach to SEC]** — Heritage Bank reported a data breach to the Securities and Exchange Commission on March 20, 2026, with class action lawyers now investigating the incident. (Source: [ClassAction.org](https://www.classaction.org/data-breach-lawsuits/heritage-bank-march-2026), 2026-03-20)
